title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 2)
text:
Governing Board of TrusteesCollege CouncilIntegrated Planning and Budget CommitteeAdministrative Services CouncilInstruction CouncilStudent Services CouncilAcademic SenateAdministrative Support Management SenateAssociated Student BoardClassified SenateCurriculum CommitteeCOS Foundation Board of DirectorsMISSION STATEMENT: We are a comprehensive community college serving the economic, educational, lifelong learning, and workforce needs of Siskiyou County and the other communities our programs reach. College of the Siskiyous provides students a strong education leading to associate degrees, certificates, college transfer, career and technical education, workforce training, and basic skills preparation. We understand the transformative power of higher education and seek to inspire students by offering inclusive and practical learning experiences that are relevant in today’s global economy and environment. We strive to prepare our students to align their newly-gained skills and experiences with those best suited to the work they wish to pursue and the lives they wish to lead. At the same time, we aim to inspire our students to engage actively, question deeply, reason critically, think boldly, communicate effectively, act innovatively, work collaboratively, and live ethically. -- Adopted June 2019, Modified April 2021VISION STATEMENT: Transforming students' lives through lifelong learning and success. Diversity Statement, Mission, Vision, and Institutional Goals can be found here: https://www.siskiyous.edu/mainfiles/diversity_mission_vision.htm It is the policy of College of the Siskiyous not to discriminate on the basis of race, color, national origin, sex or disability in its educational programs and its employment practices. (https://www.siskiyous.edu/humanresources/nondiscrimination.htm and https://www.siskiyous.edu/mainfiles/titleIX.htm)________________________________________________________________________________________________________________For technical assistance with this site, please contact BoardDocs support at 800-407-0141, option 4 or support@boarddocs.com
For more information, please contact President's Office.

BookSiskiyou Joint Community College District Administrative Procedures
SectionChapter 5: Student Services
TitleNon-Resident Tuition and Exceptions
CodeAP 5020
StatusActive
Last RevisedMay 7, 2025
Last ReviewedMarch 22, 2023
Prior Revised Dates11/09, 1/24/2012, 2/12/2013, 8/12/2014, 12/8/2015, 7/11/2023, 10/18/2023

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 6)
text: Non-resident students shall be charged non-resident tuition for all units enrolled. Non-residents are determined according to the process in AP 5015. Non-resident Tuition A Non-resident Tuition Fee based on the number of units of enrollment shall be charged to non-resident students. Exceptions are defined in Education Code and included in AP 5020 for:Oregon Exchange and Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE) StudentsInternational Student who are on the Fee ReductionAny students, other than persons excluded from the term "immigrant" for purposes of the federal Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S. Code Section 1101(a)(15)), who meet the following requirements:either high school attendance in California for three or more years OR attainment of credits earned in California from a California high school equivalent to three or more years of full-time high school coursework and a total of three or more years of attendance in California elementary schools, California secondary schools, or combination of those schools;graduation from a California high school or attainment of the equivalent thereof;registration or enrollment in a course offered for any term commencing on or after January 1, 2002;filing a California Nonresident Tuition Exemption Request form with the District or in the case of students applying for state-based financial aid, by filing a California Dream Act Application (CADAA) with the California Student Aid Commission verifying eligibility for this nonresident tuition exemption; andin the case of undocumented persons, the filing of an affidavit that the student has filed an application to legalize his/her immigration status, or will file an application as soon as he/she is eligible to do so.Any students who meet the following requirements:demonstrates financial need;has a parent who has been deported or was permitted to depart voluntarily;moved abroad as a result of that deportation or voluntary departure;lived in California immediately before moving abroad;attended a public or private secondary school in the state for three or more years; and,upon enrollment, will be in his or her first academic year as a matriculated student in California Any persons granted “T” or “U” visa status under title 8 U.S.

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 7)
text: Code Section 1101(a)(15)(T)(i) or (ii), or section 1101(a)(15)(U)(i) or (ii), respectively, who meet all the following requirements:high school attendance in California for three or more years;graduation from a California high school or attainment of the equivalent thereof;registration or enrollment in a course offered for any term or commencing on or after January 1, 2002; and in the completion of a questionnaire form prescribed by the State Chancellor's Office verifying eligibility for this nonresident tuition exemption andfiling a California Nonresident Tuition Exemption Request form with the District or in the case of students applying for state-based financial aid, by filing a California Dream Act Application (CADAA) with the California Student Aid Commission verifying eligibility for this nonresident tuition exemption Non-resident Tuition CalculationNot later than February 1, the Superintendent shall bring to the Board for approval an action to establish non-resident tuition for the following fiscal year. The fee shall be calculated in accordance with guidelines contained in applicable state regulations and/or the California Community College Attendance Accounting Manual. Non-resident Tuition and RefundsA student will receive a refund for the tuition associated with the number of units by which the student has reduced his/her program in accordance with the schedule below. This does not include fees that are forfeited on the first day of the term. The student must be officially dropped from a class to be eligible for a refund. A prorated rate will apply for short-term and late-starting classes. Oregon Exchange and WUE students do not qualify for this extended refund. This table reflects refund percentages for full term, primary-semester courses only. Week of Semester Student is Officially Dropped From Class Refund Percent Prior to Beginning of Semester 100%First two weeks up to the refund date100%Through the official refund date100%1st week after refund date75%2nd week after the refund date 50%3rd week after the refund date 25% After the above has passed, NO REFUNDS WILL BE GIVEN. (Non-resident tuition refunds for classes less than full-term length will be prorated according to the above schedule.) International Students See “Non-resident Tuition Calculation”. Exceptions to International Student TuitionThe College may exempt from all or part of the fee any non-resident who is both a citizen and resident of a foreign country (AP 5012), provided that the non-resident has demonstrated financial need for the exemption and not more than 10 percent of the non-resident foreign students are exempted. Oregon Exchange Agreement StudentsCollege of the Siskiyous has reciprocal agreements with Southern Oregon University (SOU) and Oregon Institute of Technology (OIT) to exempt Oregon student) from paying out-of-state tuition. These students shall be charged a reduced fee which is determined by Education Code 76140(j) to be one and a half times the California enrollment fee rate. Processes have been established to assure the District can claim FTES under the following conditions.

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 8)
text: Districts with fewer than 1,500 total resident and nonresident FTES may report all FTES generated under this program as resident FTES. Districts with more than 1,500 but fewer than 3,000 total resident and nonresident FTES may report up to a maximum of 100 FTES generated under this program annually as resident FTES.Any time spent at College of the Siskiyous under the auspices of this permit will not qualify toward the establishment of residence in California, i.e., it is presumed that students seeking this permit intend to retain their own state residency.a. Student will be required to re-apply for the permit if there is more than a one primary semester lapse in attendanceQualifications - SOU and OIT establish the qualifications for participation in this program. The Admissions Office at the applicable college should be contacted for information. Students must:Be an Oregon resident.High school graduate or have earned GEDORHave completed one year of academic study in good standing at an accredited institution. Conditions of PermitThis permit will remain in effect for the academic term as requested and will be automatically renewed (up to 72 units or 3 years) if the following conditions are met:Student remains in good standing at COS, which requires maintaining a 2.5 GPA and completion of 67% of course work. Student must be enrolled in a minimum of 12 units per semester. Students who need less than 12 units to finish can stay on the exchange to complete their program. One course must be in person class on either the Weed or Yreka Campus.Student must develop a Comprehensive Educational plan mapping out the completion of their goal with a Counselor and be following that plan each semester.Student must be registered for the next primary term prior to the end of the current term. (Example: Fall exchange student is registered prior to the winter break for Spring.) Students from Siskiyou County/COS attending SOU/OITCollege of the Siskiyous also has a reciprocity agreement with SOU/OIT to allow Siskiyou County or COS students to attend those colleges paying in-state tuition. The number of students participating in the program from each institution shall remain “approximately equal.” QualificationsFor OIT:Must be a California resident.Must not have any COS registration holds.Must not have a prior bachelor’s degreeMust have completed one of the following sets of requirements:Complete a minimum of 24 semester units of Associate Degree applicable courses at COS with an Associate Degree and an AA cumulative GPA of at least 2.00. ORHave been a Siskiyou County resident for at least one year. Conditions of PermitIssuance of this permit does not guarantee admission to Oregon Institute of Technology. A student must file a separate application for admission to OIT and meet OIT admission requirements.This permit will remain in effect if the following conditions are met:Student remains in good standing at OIT;Student follows prescribed course of study at OIT;Exchange agreement between College of the Siskiyous and Oregon Institute of Technology remains in effect.

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 9)
text: Any time spent at Oregon Institute of Technology under the auspices of this permit will not qualify toward the establishment of residence in Oregon, i.e., it is presumed that students seeking this permit intend to retain their California residency. For SOU:Must be a California resident.Must not have any COS registration holds.Must not have a bachelor’s degree. Must have completed one of the following sets of requirements:Complete a minimum of 24 semester units of transfer-level course work from COS and have a COS cumulative transfer GPA of at least 2.25.ORComplete an Associate degree from College of the Siskiyous with a cumulative transfer GPA of at least 2.00. Conditions of PermitIssuance of this permit does not guarantee admission to Southern Oregon University. A student must file a separate application for admission to SOU and meet SOU admission requirements.This permit may be used for undergraduate work only.This permit will remain in effect for the academic year as requested and will be automatically renewed if the following conditions are met:Student remains in good standing at SOU;Student follows prescribed course of study at SOU;Exchange agreement between College of the Siskiyous and Southern Oregon University remains in effect.Any time spent at Southern Oregon University under the auspices of this permit will not qualify toward the establishment of residence in Oregon, i.e., it is presumed that students seeking this permit intend to retain their California residency. Oregon Good Neighbor Agreement StudentsAny Oregon resident student who applies and qualifies for the Oregon Exchange program as outlined above but cannot be claimed by the District due to the 100 FTE limit will qualify for the District’s Oregon Good Neighbor program whereby the student will be charged a reduced out of state tuition fee equal to the Oregon Exchange rate plus the required California enrollment fee rate charged to non-resident students. Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE) StudentsCollege of the Siskiyous has been approved to participate in WUE Exchange program. The Western Undergraduate Exchange (WUE) is an agreement among WICHE’s 16 members, through which 160+ participating public colleges and universities provide steep nonresident tuition savings for Western students. These students shall be charged a reduced fee which is determined by Education Code 76140(j) to be one and a half the California enrollment fee rate. Processes have been established to assure the District can claim FTES under the following conditions.Districts with fewer than 1,500 total resident and nonresident FTES may report all FTES generated under this program as resident FTES. Districts with more than 1,500 but fewer than 3,000 total resident and nonresident FTES may report up to a maximum of 100 FTES generated under this program annually as resident FTES.College of the Siskiyous agrees that if at any time COS becomes disqualified under Education Code Section 76140 (h) or (i), COS will take responsibility for and absorb the difference for the student between non-resident tuition/fees and the WUE Rate, until the student no longer qualifies under the WUE criteria.

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 10)
text: Exchange agreement between College of the Siskiyous, and WUE will be reviewed each academic year prior to the Fall Start date.Any time spent at College of the Siskiyous under the auspices of this permit will not qualify toward the establishment of residence in California, i.e., it is presumed that students seeking this permit intend to retain their own state residency.a. Student will be required to re-apply for the permit if there is more than a one primary semester lapse in attendance. Qualifications: Students must:Be a resident of Alaska, Arizona, Colorado, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, New Mexico, North Dakota, Oregon, South Dakota, Utah, Washington, Wyoming or US Pacific territories and Freely Associated statesBe in at least 1 on-campus course.Be in a Matriculating major (AAT, AST, AA, AS, COA). Conditions of PermitThis permit will remain in effect for the academic term as requested and will be automatically renewed (up to 72 units or 3 years) if the following conditions are met:Student remains in good standing at COS, which requires maintaining a 2.5 GPA and completion of 67% of course work. Student must be enrolled in a minimum of 12 units per semester. Students who need less than 12 units to finish can stay on the exchange to complete their program. One course must be in person class on either the Weed or Yreka Campus.Student must develop a Comprehensive Educational plan mapping out the completion of their goal with a Counselor and be following that plan each semester.Student must be registered for the next primary term prior to the end of the current term. (Example: Fall exchange student is registered prior to the winter break for Spring.) This Administrative Procedure was reviewed and approved by Superintendent/President Dr. Char Perlas on May 7, 2025.

title: AP 5020 - AP 5020 (Part 11)
text:
